LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SALLY UNTERBERGER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED - SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CLAIMS THAT ARE THE SUBJECT OF THEM MOTION EXCEPT FOR THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY, WHICH IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FAILURE TO ATTACH EXHIBITS REQUIRES A NEW COMPLAINT. FURTHER, THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PARTITION STILL DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY PLEAD COMPLIANCE WITH THE CONDITIONS OF THE CONTRACT FOR THE PROPERTY TO BE SOLD. THERE IS NO BASIS FOR THE ALLEGATION THAT THE DEFENDANT HAS A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP WITH THE PLAINTIFF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DAVID GUERDAT'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SALLY UNTERBERGER IS ORDERED TO APPEAR TO COMPLETE HER DEPOSITION, IF SHE HAS NOT ALREADY DONE SO, ON A WEEK SELECTED BY PLAINTIFF BETWEEN THE DATE OF THIS ORDER AND OCTOBER 4, 2013. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL ARE ORDERED TO PAY SANCTIONS OF $1000.00 PURSUANT TO CCP 2023.030(A), 2023.010(D) AND 2025.450(G)(1). DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL DID NOT ACT WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION IN DELAYING THE RESCHEDULING OF THE DEFENDANT'S DEPOSITION, AFTER IT WAS SUSPENDED TWO HOURS IN TO THE DEPOSITION--AT THE REQUEST OF DEFENDANT--ON ACCOUNT OF HER FEELING TOO ILL TO CONTINUE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SALLY A. UNTERBERGER'S GENERAL AND SPECIAL DEMURRERS TO COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION (PARTITION) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT HE COMPLIED WITH THE CONTRACTED AGREEMENT IN THE T.I.C.A. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION (DECLARATORY RELIEF) IS OVERRULED. AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY EXISTS. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION (WASTE) IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED FACTS TO SUPPORT THIS CLAIM. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION (INDEMNITY) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD ACTUAL LOSSES THAT HAVE ACCRUED FOR THE MAINTENANCE OF THE PROPERTY. HOWEVER, INDEMNITY CANNOT BE USED AS TO LOSSES NOT REALIZED FROM THE LAWSUIT. (DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF DAVID GUERDAT'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: (1) INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT SHALL BE ENTERED; (2) THE COURT SHALL NOT APPOINT ONE PERSON TO SERVE AS REFEREE AND LISTING AGENT; (3) HON. HENRY LOW SHALL BE APPOINTED REFEREE, SUBJECT TO PLAINTIFF'S ORAL OPPOSITION; (4) REFEREE SHALL HIRE A LISTING AGENT; (5) 7 OF THE PROPOSED INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT SHALL REMAIN; (6) ALL CLAIMS HAVE BEEN RELEASED BY THE PARTIES IN THE SETTLEMENT. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO INCLUDE ACCOUNTING PROCEDURE IN THE JUDGMENT IS DENIED; (7) PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO RECOVER ATTORNEY'S FEES PER THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. THE AMOUNT OF SUCH FEES TO BE DETERMINED BY MOTION. ORDER FOR THE SAME SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COUNSEL TO SUBMIT BRIEFS IN REGARDS TO THE EXECUTION OF THE JUDGEMENT. JUDGE RONALD E. Off Calendar Jan-28-2015 Continued To Mar-04-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Referee Justice Harry Low, of JAMS, (415)982-5267. Atty. Michael McConathy of Lynch, Gilard and Grummer, (415) 397-2800, appearing for Defendant, the Trust of Unterberger. Atty. G. Ryan Patrick of Wiegel Law Group, (415)552-8230, appearing for Plaintiff David Guerdat. Real Estate agent Michael Kasolas, of Stevensen, Neal and Kasolas, (415)850-9525. Realtor Sandy Comaroto, of Alain Pinel, (650)714-1359, appearing for bidder Emporio Group represented by Dimitrious Sogos.

Justice Low informs the Court that this is a report of sale of real property, futhermore Justice Low conducts the sale and no overbids are presented. The Court determines Emporio Group is the winning bidder with the purchase price of $1,750,000.00. The Court and counsel discuss the duties of the referee, set the date of February 9, 2015 to complete the sale and transfer of real property, attorneys fees, and the final accounting shall be heard on March 4, 2015 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 501.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: Motion For Order Confirming Sale Of Real Property is granted. Order for the same signed in open court.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: N. Wong; Dept.501; Not Reported

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. G. Ryan Patrick and Andrew Wiegell (415)552-8230) appearing for Plaintiff David Guerdat and moving party, Atty. Mike McConathy (415)956-8100 appearing for Defendant Sally Unterberger and opposing party.

ARGUED - Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF DAVID GUERDAT'S Motion To Enforce Settlement is GRANTED as follows: (1) interlocutory judgment shall be entered; (2) the Court shall not appoint one person to serve as referee and listing agent; (3) Hon. Henry Low shall be appointed referee, subject to Plaintiff's oral opposition; (4) referee shall hire a listing agent; (5) 7 of the proposed Interlocutory Judgment shall remain; (6) all claims have been released by the parties in the Settlement. Defendant's request to include accounting procedure in the judgment is denied; (7) Plaintiff is entitled to recover attorney's fees per the settlement agreement. HEARING - R U L I N G: Defendant SALLY UNTERBERGER'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents And Things is argued and granted. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: William E. Shapiro; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

Plaintiff David Guerdat'S Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ryan Patrick, Esq. of Wiegel Law Group, Tel. 415.552.8230, for plaintiff and moving party;
Michael D. McConathy, Esq. of Lynch Gilardi & Grummer, Tel. 415.397.2800, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF DAVID GUERDAT's Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Sanctions is granted. Defendant Sally Unterberger is ordered to appear to complete her deposition, if she has not already done so, on a week selected by Plaintiff between the date of this order and October 4, 2013. Defendant's counsel are ordered to pay sanctions of $1000.00 pursuant to CCP 2023.030(a), 2023.010(d) and 2025.450(g)(1). Defendant's counsel did not act with substantial justification in delaying the rescheduling of the Defendant's deposition, after it was suspended two hours in to the deposition--at the request of Defendant--on account of her feeling too ill to continue. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James B. Kraus, Esq., from Zacks & Utrecht, 415-956-8100, for Defendant and moving party

No other appearance. Tentative ruling is adopted.
DEFENDANT SALLY UNTERBERGER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend as to all claims that are the subject of them motion except for the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary, which is sustained without leave to amend. The failure to attach exhibits requires a new complaint. Further, the first cause of action for partition still does not sufficiently plead compliance with the conditions of the contract for the property to be sold. There is no basis for the allegation that the defendant has a fiduciary relationship with the plaintiff. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SALLY A. UNTERBERGER'S GENERAL AND SPECIAL DEMURRERS TO COMPLAINT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's demurrer to the 1st cause of action (partition) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts that he complied with the contracted agreement in the T.I.C.A. Defendant's demurrer to the 4th cause of action (declaratory relief) is overruled. An actual controversy exists. Defendant's demurrer to the 5th cause of action (waste) is overruled. Plaintiff has pled facts to support this claim. Defendant's demurrer to the 7th cause of action (indemnity) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead actual losses that have accrued for the maintenance of the property. However, indemnity cannot be used as to losses not realized from the lawsuit. (Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SALLY A. UNTERBERGER'S MOTION TO STRIKE (COMPLAINT).

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's motion to strike is granted. Plaintiff did not oppose. (Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
